content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the differences between mixtures and compounds and methods of separating mixtures.

indicators.statement: Distinguish between elements, compounds and mixtures using their properties and give examples of each from everyday life.
exemplars: Classify substances such as salt, air, water and iron into elements, mixtures and compounds.
Compare the properties of mixtures and compounds using a table.

indicators.statement: Describe and demonstrate methods of separating mixtures (filtration, evaporation, distillation, chromatography, magnetism, decanting) and select appropriate methods for given mixtures.
exemplars: Separate a mixture of sand and salt using filtration and evaporation.
Use chromatography to separate ink colours and record observations.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the structure of atoms and the organisation of elements in the Periodic Table.

indicators.statement: Describe the structure of an atom (protons, neutrons, electrons) and explain the concept of atomic number, mass number and electronic configuration for the first 20 elements.
exemplars: Draw atomic diagrams showing electron arrangement for the first 20 elements.
Calculate the number of protons, neutrons and electrons given atomic and mass numbers.

indicators.statement: Explain the organisation of the Periodic Table into groups and periods and relate an element's position to its properties and electronic configuration.
exemplars: Identify the group and period of elements from their electronic configuration.
Predict properties of elements based on their position in the Periodic Table.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of how atoms combine through ionic and covalent bonding to form compounds.

indicators.statement: Explain ionic bonding as the transfer of electrons between metals and non-metals and covalent bonding as the sharing of electrons between non-metals, drawing dot-and-cross diagrams.
exemplars: Draw dot-and-cross diagrams for NaCl and H₂O to show bonding.
Compare the properties of ionic and covalent compounds (melting point, conductivity, solubility).

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the carbon cycle and its importance in maintaining the balance of carbon in the atmosphere.

indicators.statement: Describe the carbon cycle, identifying the processes that add carbon to and remove carbon from the atmosphere (combustion, respiration, photosynthesis, decomposition).
exemplars: Draw and label a diagram of the carbon cycle.
Explain how human activities (burning fossil fuels, deforestation) disrupt the carbon cycle.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the human reproductive system, the process of reproduction and stages of human development.

indicators.statement: Identify and describe the structure and functions of the male and female reproductive organs and explain the process of fertilisation and implantation.
exemplars: Label diagrams of the male and female reproductive systems.
Describe the journey of sperm from production to fertilisation of the ovum.

indicators.statement: Describe the stages of foetal development, the role of the placenta and the process of birth, and explain the importance of prenatal care.
exemplars: Create a timeline showing the key stages of human development from fertilisation to birth.
Discuss the importance of prenatal care for the health of mother and baby.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the three types of rocks and the rock cycle.

indicators.statement: Classify rocks into igneous, sedimentary and metamorphic, describe how each type is formed and explain the rock cycle.
exemplars: Examine rock samples and classify them by type.
Draw and label the rock cycle showing the conversion between rock types.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the structure and functions of the human circulatory system and its importance in maintaining life.

indicators.statement: Describe the components of the circulatory system (heart, blood vessels, blood) and explain the functions of each in transporting substances around the body.
exemplars: Label a diagram of the heart showing chambers, valves and major blood vessels.
Compare the structure and function of arteries, veins and capillaries.

indicators.statement: Describe the composition of blood (red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets, plasma) and explain the function of each component.
exemplars: Create a diagram showing the components of blood and their functions.
Explain the role of white blood cells in fighting infections.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the structure and functions of the human respiratory system and the process of gaseous exchange.

indicators.statement: Identify the organs of the respiratory system and describe the process of breathing (inhalation and exhalation) and gaseous exchange in the alveoli.
exemplars: Label a diagram of the respiratory system.
Explain how oxygen and carbon dioxide are exchanged in the alveoli.

indicators.statement: Explain the harmful effects of smoking on the respiratory system and describe ways to maintain a healthy respiratory system.
exemplars: Research the diseases caused by smoking (lung cancer, bronchitis, emphysema).
Design an anti-smoking campaign poster for the school.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of ecosystems, food chains, food webs and the interdependence of organisms.

indicators.statement: Define an ecosystem and identify its components (biotic and abiotic factors), construct food chains and food webs from given organisms.
exemplars: Identify biotic and abiotic factors in the school compound ecosystem.
Construct a food web for a Ghanaian grassland or forest ecosystem.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of heat transfer by conduction, convection and radiation and their applications in daily life.

indicators.statement: Explain the three methods of heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation), identify materials as conductors or insulators and describe everyday applications.
exemplars: Conduct experiments to compare heat conduction in different materials.
Explain how convection currents cause land and sea breezes.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the properties of light including reflection, refraction and the formation of images.

indicators.statement: Describe the properties of light (travels in straight lines, reflection, refraction) and use ray diagrams to show reflection in plane mirrors and refraction through rectangular glass blocks.
exemplars: Draw ray diagrams showing the laws of reflection.
Demonstrate refraction by placing a pencil in a glass of water and explaining the observation.

indicators.statement: Explain how lenses (convex and concave) form images and describe their applications in everyday life (spectacles, cameras, magnifying glasses).
exemplars: Draw ray diagrams showing image formation by convex and concave lenses.
Explain how the eye uses a convex lens to form images and how spectacles correct defects.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the production, transmission and properties of sound energy.

indicators.statement: Explain how sound is produced by vibrations, describe how it travels through different media and explain the concepts of pitch, loudness and echo.
exemplars: Investigate how changing the length or tension of a string affects the pitch of sound.
Explain why sound travels faster in solids than in gases.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of forces, their effects on objects and the relationship between force, mass and acceleration.

indicators.statement: Identify types of forces (gravitational, frictional, elastic, magnetic, electrostatic) and describe their effects on objects (change in shape, speed, direction).
exemplars: Demonstrate the effects of different forces on everyday objects.
Measure forces using a spring balance and express results in Newtons.

indicators.statement: Explain the concepts of speed, velocity and acceleration, calculate speed using the formula speed = distance/time and interpret distance-time graphs.
exemplars: Calculate the speed of a vehicle given distance and time.
Plot and interpret distance-time graphs for objects moving at constant speed.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of chemical reactions, including signs of chemical change and types of reactions.

indicators.statement: Identify signs of chemical reactions (colour change, gas production, temperature change, precipitate formation) and distinguish between physical and chemical changes.
exemplars: Observe and record signs of chemical reactions (e.g., vinegar + bicarbonate of soda).
Classify given changes as physical or chemical with reasons.

indicators.statement: Write word equations for simple chemical reactions and classify reactions as combination, decomposition, displacement and neutralisation.
exemplars: Write word equations for reactions observed in experiments.
Classify given chemical reactions by type and explain the pattern.
